Event: Reins in Tony’s impulses and supports him as consigliere
Explanation: Strengthens his own position and the family’s stability while somewhat restraining Tony’s excesses.

Event: Maintains loyalty and trust so Tony can completely rely on him
Explanation: Full loyalty preserves his safety and influence while giving Tony a uniquely dependable ally.

Event: Refuses to pry when Tony may have killed a made man over a horse
Explanation: Protects himself and Tony’s authority at the cost of the code and collective interests.

Event: Runs Bada Bing as abusive pimp, using girls as terrorized prostitutes
Explanation: Generates substantial income and power by heavily exploiting and terrorizing the women.

Event: Is physically abusive toward Tracee for skipping work and talking back
Explanation: Maintains control and income through violence while seriously harming her.

Event: Coldly insults and executes Adriana as she begs for her life
Explanation: Removes an informant and demonstrates loyalty/power at the cost of her life.

Event: Personally participates in executing FBI informant Jimmy Altieri
Explanation: Eliminates a traitor and raises his standing while harming Jimmy and his family.

Event: Personally participates in executing Big Pussy for informing
Explanation: Gains safety and status by removing a dangerous mole, harming the victim.

Event: Personally participates in executing Adriana for informing
Explanation: Protects the organization and himself, gravely harming Adriana.

Event: Bans Ralphie from the Bing for life after he beats Tracy to death
Explanation: Reasserts house rules and respect; minor personal risk but improves internal norms and image.

Event: Is elated when Tony tells him to personally deal with Coach Hauser
Explanation: Seeks satisfaction and status from punishing a pedophile, with extra-legal harm to target.

Event: Gets visibly pissed when Tony later calls off the hit on Coach Hauser
Explanation: Frustration and loss of desired action; others are spared more violence and escalation.

Event: Lies in wait to murder Meadow’s soccer coach over abuse revelation
Explanation: Prepares to avenge perceived harm and uphold family code via extrajudicial killing.

Event: Leads a furious crusade against Native protesters over Columbus Day
Explanation: Incurs big headaches for Tony and himself while antagonizing others with no real gain.

Event: Shows hair-trigger temper and insults people when gambling goes bad
Explanation: Damages relationships and reputation from anger without material benefit.

Event: Throws insults and kicks dirt at a referee at his daughter’s game
Explanation: Hurts his own image and risks trouble while providing no real advantage to anyone.

Event: Fails under stress as acting boss, has asthma attack and is hospitalized
Explanation: Accepts top role then physically collapses from stress, harming himself more than others.

Event: Serves as acting boss while Tony is in a coma and makes poor decisions
Explanation: Suffers near-breakdown and damages crew morale and approval with mismanagement.

Event: Counsels Tony to hand over Tony Blundetto to avoid wider mob war
Explanation: Protects himself and the family from escalation despite sacrificing an associate.

Event: Strongly recommends Tony reject Vito’s return to prevent internal strife
Explanation: Maintains his and Tony’s standing and reduces risk of violent splits in the ranks.

Event: Refuses to tolerate pedophiles within his moral code
Explanation: Enhances his internal and social standing while targeting widely condemned behavior.

Event: Rejects idea of a bomb scare at an informant’s wake as too disrespectful
Explanation: Moderates excess cruelty, preserving some norms with little cost to himself.

Event: Objects to Tony’s nepotism in making Christopher acting capo
Explanation: Risks minor disfavor to push for competent leadership benefitting the crew.

Event: Is less bothered than Tony but still repulsed by Ralphie’s killing of Tracy
Explanation: Recognizes line-crossing, reinforcing minimal standards without direct personal loss.

Event: Intends only to beat Fat Dom and send him on his way for insults
Explanation: Plans moderate retaliation to maintain respect with limited harm relative to norms.

Event: Feels Fat Dom doesn’t deserve death and is disturbed when Carlo kills him
Explanation: Experiences guilt and stress while at least wanting to limit excess killing.

Event: Tries to keep Tony from seeing Fat Dom’s body to avoid involving him
Explanation: Attempts to shield Tony legally and politically while limiting wider fallout.

Event: Hustles Tony out, telling him “you don’t know nothin’!” over Dom’s body
Explanation: Reduces Tony’s exposure and culpability with negligible cost to himself.

Event: Is content to simply beat Fat Dom instead of killing him
Explanation: Aims for proportionate response that preserves respect while avoiding full escalation.

Event: Sanctions Patsy Parisi’s insubordination in “No Show”
Explanation: Vents frustration at Tony but undermines hierarchy, risking backlash without net benefit.

Event: Leads major Columbus Day protest actions against Native activists
Explanation: Stirs conflict and problems for Tony and himself for purely symbolic payoff.

Event: Gets into serious conflict with Tony over Columbus Day issue
Explanation: Strains relationship with his protector and boss with no real strategic gain.

Event: Counsels Tony firmly but politely over Tony’s pride (“What the Hell, Hero?”)
Explanation: Risks mild offense to give honest feedback that benefits Tony and the organization.

Event: Acts as living lie detector, sensing Tony lies about Christopher’s death but staying silent
Explanation: Protects himself by not confronting Tony while keeping accurate read for future advice.

Event: Snarks at Tony and others, including about Vito’s sexuality
Explanation: Gets social capital and humor at others’ expense, mildly harming targets’ dignity.

Event: Politely calls out Tony’s nepotism and bad decisions
Explanation: Improves decision-making climate while incurring modest interpersonal risk.

Event: Tries to rein in random violence among more unstable mobsters
Explanation: Improves group safety and income while solidifying his role as stabilizer.

Event: Refuses to join New York’s conspiracy against Tony
Explanation: Preserves loyalty and self-respect while avoiding a treacherous, risky move.

Event: Undermines Tony’s authority once, via stealing floor tiles as a “message”
Explanation: Risks trust and position for a petty symbolic act with limited practical benefit.

Event: Pays Tony part of the money made from selling stolen floor tiles
Explanation: Reduces damage to trust and returns some gain, smoothing over conflict.

Event: Reads Tony flawlessly over time and adjusts advice accordingly
Explanation: Enhances his influence and Tony’s decision quality, helping overall outcomes.

Event: Refuses to judge Tony for attending therapy and is supportive
Explanation: Strengthens friendship and trust while supporting Tony’s mental health.

Event: Is extremely loyal friend to Tony outside strictly criminal matters
Explanation: Gains emotional support and deep alliance while benefitting Tony’s personal life.

Event: Tries to keep Tony from being involved in the aftermath of Fat Dom’s death
Explanation: Reduces Tony’s exposure and possible legal risk with little downside to himself.

Event: Participates in killing suspected rats beyond confirmed cases
Explanation: Protects himself and the crew’s secrecy while often harming possibly innocent targets.

Event: Violently slaps strippers to keep them under control and productive
Explanation: Maintains revenue and dominance through fear, seriously harming the women involved.

Event: Frequently cheats on his wife with his own workers
Explanation: Gains personal pleasure and ego at the emotional expense of family and employees.

Event: Refuses to tolerate bomb-scare prank at wake for traitor
Explanation: Limits gratuitous cruelty and bad optics while losing nothing significant.

Event: Supports Tony as he handles Livia’s wake despite resenting missing Jets game
Explanation: Accepts personal annoyance to meet social/organizational obligations.

Event: Refuses to join Lupertazzi side when Burt Gervasi invites him
Explanation: Stays loyal and avoids being on losing/treacherous side, though it puts him at some risk.

Event: Calmly agrees to meet Burt at his home after Burt says he’s switching sides
Explanation: Sets up a situation where he will control the encounter and its outcome.

Event: Brutally strangles Burt Gervasi to death in his own home
Explanation: Eliminates a turncoat and asserts loyalty, but intensifies the war and personal danger.

Event: Updates Tony about killing Burt, confirming his loyalty and action
Explanation: Aligns narratives, reinforces trust, and helps Tony coordinate next moves.

Event: Helps “keep the ship moving” through hyper-competent advising over years
Explanation: Enjoys security and status by continually stabilizing operations and strategy.

Event: Takes over as acting boss in “Mayham” despite not wanting role
Explanation: Accepts duty that severely harms his health despite temporarily helping continuity.

Event: Shows overt racism and homophobia, praising Richie for disowning gay son
Explanation: Gains social approval in his subculture at clear expense of stigmatized groups.

Event: Treats women (other than wife and daughter) horribly and misogynistically
Explanation: Extracts labor, sex, and control while strongly degrading their wellbeing.

Event: Stresses over possibly having to take over for Tony as boss
Explanation: Experiences anxiety and health consequences while others benefit from his diligence.

Event: Sees a therapist at least once for his issues
Explanation: Invests in mental health, improving his functioning and indirectly benefiting associates.

Event: Buys and wears toupee to improve appearance and gravitas
Explanation: Boosts his status and confidence, slightly improving how others view and work with him.

Event: Builds himself into a self-made owner of Bada Bing and related ventures
Explanation: Increases wealth and status while providing economic activity, despite illicit context.

Event: Has a hand in the music industry alongside his criminal work
Explanation: Diversifies income and creates value for collaborators and artists.

Event: Maintains close, loving marriage with Gabriella despite infidelity
Explanation: Derives emotional stability and support while providing a relatively caring partnership overall.

Event: Cares deeply for his daughter and dotes on her as “The Principessa”
Explanation: Finds meaning and joy in fatherhood while strongly supporting his child’s wellbeing.

Event: Shows papa-wolf protectiveness over his daughter’s team situation
Explanation: Overprotective but primarily motivated to secure her environment.

Event: Becomes enraged and insulting when gambling or competition goes badly
Explanation: Undermines his own composure and relationships without gaining advantage.

Event: Regularly attends to Tony’s needs as right-hand man and confidant
Explanation: Secures his own safety and influence while making Tony’s leadership more effective.

Event: Is nearly killed in Lupertazzi hit and falls into likely permanent coma
Explanation: Attackers incur war escalation and Sil loses almost everything, benefitting nobody clearly.
